title: NOC 31303 – Physician Assistants, Midwives and Allied Health Professionals | GTR Immigration
description: Physician Assistants, Midwives, and Allied Health Professionals under NOC 31303 are in strong demand across the healthcare system in Canada. Workforce shortages, expansion of community-based care, and increased healthcare accessibility have created sustained opportunities for internationally trained professionals in this TEER 1 occupation. With high earning potential, structured regulatory pathways, and strong immigration eligibility, NOC 31303 offers a strategic route toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group31 – Professional occupations in healthSub-Major Group313 – Nursing and allied health professionalsMinor Group3130 – Nursing and allied health professionalsNOC Code31303

duties: This group performs some or all of the following duties:
Conduct patient interviews and physical assessments
Develop treatment plans under physician supervision
Provide full-course prenatal, labour, delivery, and postnatal care
Conduct physical examinations and diagnostic monitoring
Monitor newborn health and provide infant care guidance
Assess genetic risk and family history
Coordinate genetic testing and follow-up plans
Diagnose and treat binocular vision disorders
Assist with autopsies and specimen examinations
employment_requirements: Completion of a university degree in a physician assistant program
Registration required in Manitoba and New Brunswick
Registration required in Nova Scotia, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, Alberta, British Columbia, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut
Certification with Canadian Association of Genetic Counsellors
Master’s degree in pathologists’ assistant program
Certification from recognized bodies including the Canadian Certification Council of Pathologists’ Assistants
Membership in Canadian Association of Pathologists may be required
Internationally educated professionals must complete credential recognition and provincial registration where applicable.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range between $58,100 and $121,300, depending on province, specialization, and experience.
job_outlook: The employment outlook for NOC 31303 remains stable nationwide. Demand is driven by:
Growing maternal and newborn care needs
Growth in diagnostic and pathology services
Internationally educated professionals who complete certification and provincial registration have strong long-term employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 31303 are employed in:
This balanced outlook reflects stable demand, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Manitoba, Quebec, and Atlantic regions.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 31303?
Expanding scope of practice in many provinces
Healthcare remains a priority sector in Canada’s immigration strategy.
pathways: If you are a qualified Physician Assistant, Midwife, or Allied Health Professional, NOC 31303 provides a structured and high-demand immigration pathway to Canada.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your professional journey toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 31303.
